original: St Eutychian begins his reign as Catholic Pope
Battle at Reading: Ethelred of Wessex defeated by Danish invasion army
Viking Great Heathen Army defeats a Wessex force led by King Æthelred and his brother Alfred at Reading
Anna of Brittany announces that all those who would ally with the king of France will be considered guilty of the crime of lese-majesty
Spanish viceroy Alva banishes Zutphen City's only physician, Joost Sweiter, "because he is a Jew"
Spanish galleon Nuestra Señora de las Maravillas, laden with gold and jewellery bound for Spain, sinks off the coast of the Bahamas, only 45 of 650 people survive (most eaten by sharks)
Most of the Palace of Whitehall in London, the main residence of the English monarchs, is destroyed by fire
Netherlands, Great Britain & France sign Triple Alliance
Benjamin Franklinarrives in London [OS=Dec 24 1724]
Columbia University founded, as Kings College (NYC)
Seven Years' War: Great Britain declares war on Spain & Naples
Snowstorm hitsGeorge Washington's army at Morristown, New Jersey
Andre Méchain discovers M80 (globular cluster in Scorpio)
Insurrection of Trinidad negroes
Royal Academy (Technical Hague court) Delft opens
The McDonald Islands are discovered by Captain William McDonald aboard the Samarang
US Civil War: Federal Ft Morgan in Mobile, Alabama seized by Confederate forces
Four-wheeled roller skates are patented by James Plimpton in New York
The New Apostolic Church is established in Germany
New York Stock Exchange opens its first permanent headquarters at 10-12 Broad St, near Wall Street in New York City
Wilkie Collins' "The Moonstone" is first serialized in "All the Year Round" owned by Charles Dickins
Sofia is emancipated from Ottoman rule
Ontario Rugby Football Union (forerunner of CFL) forms
Last sighting of an eastern cougar (Ontario)
The Fabian Society is founded in London, Egland to promote equality, citizenship, and human rights.
Dr William Grant of Iowa, performs 1st appendectomy in America on Mary Gartside, aged 22
Thomas Stevens completes the first bicycle trip around the world, arriving back in San Francisco after 2 years and 9 months
US President Cleveland grants amnesty to Mormon polygamy
France ratifies Duple Alliance with Russia
American Federation of Labor (AFL) charters Actors' National Protective Union, NYC
Following Mormon abandonment of sanctioned polygamy, Utah is admitted as 45th US state
First installment of William Dean Howells' "Life and Letters" appears
The French Panama Canal Company offers to sell its right to build a canal to the US for 40 million, tipping the balance away from those favoring a canal through Nicaragua

In 'Gonzales v Williams', the US Supreme Court rules that Puerto Ricans are not aliens and may not be refused admission into continental United States; not until 1917 will citizenship rights be granted
Stanley Cup, Aberdeen Pavilion, Ottawa, Ontario: Ottawa HC beats Winnipeg Rowing Club, 2-0 for 2-1 challenge series victory
South Africa beat England by one wicket, their 1st Test win
Smallest earth-moon distance this century, 356,375 km center-to-center
The Scout Association is incorporated throughout the British Commonwealth by Royal Charter
First elected Jewish US governor, Moses Alexander, takes office in Idaho
Trans-Caucausus Russian defeat Turkish troops
Amsterdam actors decide to strike for retirement benefits
First broadcast of "Barn Dance Show" (WBAP - Fort Worth, Texas)
French psychologist Emil Coué brings his self-esteem therapy to US "Every day in every way I am getting better & better"
Theodoros Pangalos resigns as Greek dictator
State of siege proclaimed in Honduras
Premiere of the first Dutch talkie movie, Jan Teunissen's "Willem van Oranje"
Ft Jefferson National Monument, Florida established
Billboard magazine publishes its first music hit parade
Frieda Wunderlich is elected as the first woman dean of a US graduate school
Resistance fighters Honoré d'Estienne d'Orves and Jan Doornik meet for the first time at a cafe in Montparnasse, Paris, to set up a second network of the French Resistance
Operation Carpetbagger begins (aerial dropping of supplies and weapons to resistance fighters in Europe)
Germans execute resistance fighters in Amsterdam
US jeep-aircraft carrier Ommaney Bay sinks after Japanese kamikaze attack
Burma declares independence from the United Kingdom
Korean War: Chinese forces recapture Seoul
KTSM TV channel 9 in El Paso, TX (NBC) begins broadcasting
Soap opera "The Brighter Day" premieres
"Blondie" situation comedy premieres on NBC TV (later on CBS)
MLB Los Angeles Dodgers buy 44 passenger twin-engine airplane for $775,000
Sputnik 1 reenters the atmosphere and burns up
European Free Trade Association forms in Stockholm
Longest recorded strike ends as the Danish barbers' assistants end a 33 year strike
First automated (unmanned) subway train in New York City
Soviet Luna 4 reaches Earth orbit but fails to reach the Moon
Doug Walters scores second Test century in his second Test
Military coup in Upper Volta leads to a new national constitution
WFLD TV channel 32 in Chicago, IL (IND) begins broadcasting
English land and boat racer Donald Campbell is killed while driving jet-powered boat Bluebird K7 on Coniston Water in England trying to beat his own water speed record
Duck hunter accidentally shoots endangered whooping crane in Texas
Leo Fender sells Fender Guitars for $13 million to CBS
A People's Democracy march between Belfast and Derry is repeatedly attacked by loyalists and off-duty police officers
France begins arms embargo against Israel
More than 15,000 people are killed in Tonghai County, China after a magnitude 7.7 earthquake
NYC transit fare rises from 20 cents to 30 cents, new larger tokens used
Melvin H. Evans is inaugurated as the first elected Governor of the Virgin Islands
Philadelphia's multi-purpose Veterans Stadium is dedicated; it becomes home to the MLB Phillies and NFL Eagles
Rose Heilbron becomes the first female judge to sit at the Old Bailey in London
Ice thickness measured at 4776 m, Wilkes Land, Antarctica
New Orleans Jazz set a then NBA record (with 24-second shot clock) by scoring only 20 points in the first half of a 111-89 loss in Seattle; unwanted record stands for nearly 25 years

NHL Boston Bruins Dave Forbes becomes 1st athlete indicted for criminal assault for excessive violence during play; stick butt-end the face of Minnesota North Star Henry Boucha caused permanent vision damage; court case ends in hung jury, and a civil settlement is eventually reached
NHL Montreal Canadiens shutout Washington Capitals 10-0
"Home Sweet Homer" opens & closes at Palace Theater NYC
The Ulster Volunteer Force shoot dead 6 Catholic civilians in 2 coordinated attacks in County Armagh, North Ireland
Mary Shane hired by Chicago White Sox as 1st woman TV play-by-play announcer
US State of Ohio agrees to pay $675,000 to relatives of Kent State University shooting victims, in an out of court settlement

"Red Right 88", trailing 14–12 Clevelands Browns attempt an end zone pass play (Red Right 88) and a game-winning field goal in the final minute, but pass intercepted by Raiders safety Mike Davis and Oakland wins
ABC Direction Network (57 affiliates) & ABC Rock Network (40 affiliates) become the 5th & 6th ABC radio network
Bryant Gumbel becomes co-host of NBC's "Today"
Chris Wallace becomes co-anchor of NBC's "Today Show"
San Francisco Bay's Golden Gate Bridge closed for 3rd time by fierce storm
US Football League holds its 1st player draft
"Night Court," starring Harry Anderson and created by comedy writer Reinhold Weege, premieres on NBC TV; runs for 9 seasons
Adrian Dantley ties Wilt Chamberlain's NBA record for most free throws made in a game by converting 28 of 29 free throws in Utah's 116-111 win over Houston at Las Vegas
Edmonton beats Minnesota 12-8 highest-scoring modern NHL game
Sixteen die in a train crash in Chase, Maryland
Comet Tempel 1 at perihelion
US F-14s shoot down two Libyan jet fighters over the Mediterranean
307 dead and 700 injured after overloaded passenger train collides with empty freight train in Pakistan
12-year-old Chinese diver Fu Mingxia wins 10m platform gold medal at World Swimming Championships in Perth, Australia; youngest world champion in the history of any aquatic event
AT&T workers in Newark accidentally snap a cable
Iraq agrees to send Aziz to Geneva to meet Baker on Jan 9th
Jan Krzystof Bielecki becomes premier of Poland
A massive ice storm hits eastern Canada and the northeastern United States, continues through January 10, and causes widespread destruction
Wilaya of Relizane massacres in Algeria: over 170 are killed in three remote villages
Former professional wrestler Jesse Ventura is sworn in as Governor of Minnesota
Gunmen open fire on Shiite Muslims worshiping in an Islamabad mosque in Pakistan, killing 16 people and injuring 25
19 are killed after two trains collide in Asta, Norway
After 2 months, Catherine Hartley and Fiona Thornewill, reach the South Poll, the same day as Edmund Hillary 48 years before
American businessman and investor Mark Cuban purchases a majority stake in the Dallas Mavericks NBA franchise for $285m from H. Ross Perot, Jr.
Mikheil Saakashvili is elected President of Georgia
NASA Mars rover Spirit lands successfully on Mars at 04:35 UTC
Adam Gilchrist of Australia becomes the most prolific century scoring wicket keeper in cricket history when he smashes 113 from just 120 balls in the 3rd Test against Pakistan in Sydney

Phil Taylor, with a 7–1 victory in the final over Dutchman Raymond van Barneveld wins his 12th PDC World Darts title, his 14th in all; his 110.94 average remains a record for the PDC World Darts Championship final
The Burj Khalifa, world's tallest building at 829.8 m (2,722 ft), officially opens in Dubai
The US Department of Health and Human Services and the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention remove HIV infection from their list of communicable diseases of public health significance
6 Russian tourists are killed, and 2 are seriously injured when their snowmobile veers off a ski slope on Mount Cermis, Italy
A Beechcraft BE35 airplane crashes into a house in Palm Coast, Florida, in faialed emergency landing, killing 3 in the plane, no one in the home was injured
Car bomb kills 11 people, and injures 40 at a petrol station in Damascus, Syria
Gunman kills 8 people in Kawit, Philippines
14 people are killed after a three-story building construction site collapses in Goa, India
Gary Anderson of Scotland beats 16-time world champion Phil Taylor, 7-6 to win his first PDC World Darts Championship at the Alexandra Palace, London
Colombo's Gemology Institute certifies world's largest ever blue star sapphire at 1404.49 carats, found in a Sri Lankan mine in August 2015
'Bomb Cyclone' hits US Northeast prompting flooding and snow in New York subway system
Truck hits a train near Kroonstad city, South Africa killing 19 passengers

Indian cricket batsmen Cheteshwar Pujara scores 193 and Rishabh Pant 159no as the tourists declare at 622/7 on Day 2 of 4th Test against Australia in Sydney; Pant, first Indian wicketkeeper to hit a century in Australia
Thailand's worst storm in 30 years, Tropical Storm Pabuk makes landfall on country's east coast
Floods and landslides caused by torrential rain in Jakarta, Indonesia, kill at least 53
First Oxford-AstraZeneca COVID-19 vaccines are given to the general public, with 82-year-old Brian Pinker in the UK the first to receive the jab
South Korea, already the country with world's lowest birth rate, records more deaths than births for the first time
Canadian government announces US$31.5 billion settlement to fix and compensate for Indigenous child welfare system, largest in its history
Toyota becomes the first foreign automaker to top US sales, beating GM in 2021, partly due to supply issues
39 people killed in car bomb attack in Hiraan province, Somalia, with al-Qaeda linked group al-Shabab claiming responsibility
US and other countries including Italy, Japan and India re-introduce Covid testing of people arriving from China as WHO warns China under-representing Covid's true impact in the country
Julius Caesardefeats Titus Labienus in the Battle of Ruspina
Portuguese attack fails to take Calicut, the "City of Spices," on India's Malabar coast; Marshal of Portugal, Dom Fernando, is killed, and Governor of India,Afonso de Albuquerque, is injured
KingCharles I, with 400 soldiers, attacks the English Parliament
Manuscripts ofEmily Brontë's "Wuthering Heights" and AnneBrontë's "Agnes Grey" sent to publisher T.C. Newby (published December 1847)
Samuel Coltsells his first revolver pistol to the United States government
Romney Campaign:Stonewall Jacksonoccupies Bath, West Virginia
Johannes Brahms' "Academic Festival Overture" premieres in Breslau
George Bernard Shaw's play "Don Juan in Hell" premieres in London
Eugene O'Neill's play "Diff'rent" premieres in NYC
British Viceroy of India Lord Willingdon arrests Indian independence leadersMahatma GandhiandJawaharlal Nehru
Bob Hopeis first heard on network radio as part of "The Intimate Revue"
CricketerClarrie Grimmettbecomes the world record wicket taker with number 190 vs South Africa
Hermann GoeringappointsReinhard Heydrichhead of Jewish Emigration
7 x NL batting championRogers Hornsbyis 14th player selected to the Baseball Hall of Fame
British Prime MinisterWinston Churchill& US Army Chief of Staff General Marshall fly to Florida
Thomas Manncompletes his tetralogy, "Joseph & His Brothers"
Ralph Buncheis appointed as the first African American official in the US State Department
Emilio G. Segrèand Carlo Perrier announce technetium, a previously unknown element and the first artificially synthesized chemical element
Elvis Presleyrecords his second demo at Sun Studios, a recording studio in Memphis, "It Wouldn't Be The Same Without You" and "I'll Never Stand In Your Way"
NZ team led byEdmund Hillaryreaches the South Pole, the 1st to reach the Pole overland using motor vehicles and the 1st sinceAmundsenin 1911 and Scott in 1912
LBJ's "Great Society" State of the Union Address
AFL Championship, Alameda County Coliseum, Oakland: Kansas City Chiefs beat Oakland Raiders, 17-7; Chiefs defense forces 4 interceptions
Beatles last recording session at Abbey Road studios, completingGeorge Harrison's tune "I Me Mine";John Lennonwas on vacation and did not participate
Weekly CBS documentary show "The Twentieth Century" hosted byWalter Cronkiteends
US PresidentRichard Nixonrefuses to hand over tapes subpoenaed by Watergate Committee
US PresidentGerald Fordsigns Executive Order on CIA activities within the US (No 11828)
British police arrestPeter Sutcliffe, the "Yorkshire Ripper"
Eurythmicsrelease their breakthrough second studio album "Sweet Dreams (Are Made of This)"
Los Angeles Rams running backEric Dickersonscores twice as he rushes for an NFL postseason record 248 yards in 20-0 victory over Dallas Cowboys in NFC divisional playoff in Anaheim, California
NCAA basketball'sDavid Robinsonblocks a record 14 shots
George H. W. Bushis 1st sitting Vice President since Millard Filmore (in 1850) to declare himself President of the United States

Boston'sRobert Parishbecomes 5th player in NBA history to register 2,000 career blocks during Celtics' 100-96 loss at Minnesota - othersKareem Abdul-Jabbar, Mark Eaton, Wayne Rollins and George Johnson
Newt Gingrich(R) becomes speaker of the US House of Representatives
A day after accepting the head coaching position at the New York Jets,Bill Belichickresigns and moves to the New England Patriots
RapperVanilla Icespends night in jail after allegedly ripping out some of his wife's hair during a row

Washington Wizards forwardMichael Jordanbecomes the fourth player in NBA history to score 30,000 career points when he hits a free throw in the second quarter of an 89-83 win against his old team, the Chicago Bulls
Israeli Prime MinisterAriel Sharonsuffers a second, more serious stroke. His authority is transferred to acting Prime Minister Ehud Olmert.
The 110th United States Congress convenes and electsNancy Pelosias the first female Speaker of the House
Liverpool and Egyptian forwardMohamed Salahis named African Football Player of the Year
Australian bushfires death toll reaches 23 as PMScott Morrisonannounces national defense force will be employed to help fight the more than 200 fires, amid criticism of his leadership
Duhan van der Merwesigns for Worcester Warriors in the Premiership Rugby competition for the 2021-22 season
Prime MinisterBoris Johnsonannounces new national lockdown for England following Scotland, as COVID-19 variant spreads rapidly with hospitalizations now higher than the first wave
Diplomatic row erupts between India and the Maldives after Indian PMNarendra Modiencourages Indians to travel to the Lakshadweep islands instead

PresidentJoe Bidenawards the US Presidential Medal of Freedom to 19 people including chef-humanitarianJosé Andrés, politicianHillary Clinton, Vogue magazine editorAnna Wintour, actorDenzel Washington, soccer playerLionel Messi, actorMichael J. Fox, singerBono, and philanthropistGeorge Soros
First Altenburg meeting in Saxony betweenMartin Lutherand papal nuncio Karl von Miltitz
Topsy the elephant is electrocuted by her owners at Luna Park, Coney Island, and filmed by theEdison ManufacturingCompany
USSR's Luna 1 (Mechta) becomes the first spacecraft to escape Earth's gravity
Emperor Zhezong, 7th Emperor of the Song Dynasty in China (1085-1100), born in Song China (d. 1100)
Amadeus VI, "the Green Count", Count of Savoy, born in Chambéry, Holy Roman Empire (d. 1383)
